title: Exact Duplicates, Boilerplate, Similarity, Canonicals, and Change Detection · Transfer Build
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers exact duplicates, boilerplate, similarity, canonicals, and change detection into a changed content identity and change-evidence model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of content identity and change-evidence model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Keep response-byte, decoded-source, normalized-text, extracted-record, and report hashes distinct and version their transformations.
Separate repeated navigation, templates, legal text, and page-specific main content with site-tested rules and retained raw evidence.
Use documented tokenization, fingerprints, thresholds, and labeled evaluation and report uncertain similarity without claiming semantic equality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Change Classification
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-change-classification-1sv8d9" that uses case 4836 to separate byte, source, text, record, canonical, similarity, boilerplate, and change evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Change Classification.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Change Classification into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Change Classification, keep Change Classification connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-change-classification-1sv8d9
# Competency: scraper-change-classification.
# Case 4836: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly.
def evidence_scraper_change_classification_1sv8d9(fetched_pages: int = 80, exact_groups: int = 7, near_groups: int = 5, changed_pages: int = 18, unsupported_equivalence_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1sv8d9 = "1sv8d9-9d8vs1-4836"
    assert evidence_variant_1sv8d9
    evidence_axes_1sv8d9 = (
        "authorization1sv8d9",
        "url4836",
        "response9d8vs1",
        "tree1sv8d94836",
        "frontier48361sv8d9",
        "report9d8vs14836",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1sv8d9)) == 6
    if fetched_pages < 1 or exact_groups < 0 or near_groups < 0:
        return False, fetched_pages, "dedup-count"
    if changed_pages < 0 or changed_pages > fetched_pages:
        return False, changed_pages, "change-count"
    if unsupported_equivalence_claims > 0:
        return False, unsupported_equivalence_claims, "similarity-overclaim"
    if exact_groups + near_groups < 2:
        return False, exact_groups + near_groups, "dedup-evidence"
    assert changed_pages <= fetched_pages and unsupported_equivalence_claims == 0
    return True, changed_pages, "bounded-content-identity"
